votes.reason: The Python code faithfully translates the Coq definitions, including the helper functions for extracting digits, filtering odd digits, and computing their product.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, including the digit extraction, filtering of odd digits, and product calculation.
The Python code faithfully translates the Coq definitions, including the helper functions for extracting digits, filtering odd digits, and computing their product.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. get_digits_helper extracts digits via mod 10 and div 10 with the same fuel logic. get_digits uses n as fuel (sufficient for any positive n). product multiplies list elements with base 1. digits_impl filters odd digits and returns 0 if empty, otherwise their product. The precondition and spec match exactly.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. get_digits_helper extracts digits via mod 10 and div 10 with the same fuel logic. get_digits uses n as fuel (sufficient for any positive n). product computes the product of a list starting from 1. digits_impl filters odd digits and returns 0 if empty, otherwise the product. The precondition and spec match exactly. All concrete inputs will produce the same results.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. get_digits_helper extracts digits via mod 10 and div 10 with the same fuel logic. get_digits uses n as fuel (sufficient for any positive n). product multiplies list elements with base 1. digits_impl filters odd digits and returns 0 if empty, otherwise their product. The precondition and spec match exactly.
Python mirrors the Coq logic: it extracts base-10 digits with a fuel limit, filters odd digits, returns their product or 0 if none, and the pre/spec checks match for positive n.
Python mirrors Coq: it builds digits (LSB-first) with fuel=n, filters odd digits, returns their product or 0 if none; precondition n>0 and spec (equality to digits_impl) match on all positive n.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: same digit extraction with fuel=n, odd filtering, and product; precondition n>0 and spec equality are implemented identically. For nonnegative inputs, // and % match Coq nat div/mod.
